symptoms.symptom_name: Facial flame burn with singed nasal hair and eyebrows

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Facial burns and singed nasal hair are established predictors of inhalation injury and airway compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hoarse voice with throat tightness after hot-gas exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Voice change and throat tightness signal supraglottic oedema — a soft sign of impending airway obstruction.
Carbonaceous sputum is a strong marker of smoke/combustion-product inhalation and airway soot deposition.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blistering and epidermal sloughing over the anterior chest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Blistering with epidermal sloughing is characteristic of a partial-thickness (deep dermal) burn.

symptoms.symptom_name: Still able to speak in full sentences

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved phonation now does NOT exclude progressive airway oedema — a falsely reassuring feature, not a reason to delay escalation.
Body-surface-area estimation is a clinician bedside assessment; ~15% TBSA crosses the resuscitation threshold. Cannot be measured reliably via chat.
Lower-airway irritation from inhaled hot gases; auscultation is a clinician measurement not available in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia and tachypnoea (HR 115, RR 24) with SpO2 95%

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: burn with early physiological stress / hypovolaemia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain, catecholamine response and early fluid shift; measured vitals are sealed clinician data — the AI infers distress from the carer's report, not a measured number.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated carboxyhaemoglobin on arterial blood gas

investigation_details.expected_result: elevated carboxyhaemoglobin consistent with combustion-gas exposure
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: CO toxicity missed because standard SpO2 reads falsely normal.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Co-oximetry distinguishes carboxyhaemoglobin from oxyhaemoglobin; a hospital investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyponatraemia with hyperkalaemia on serum electrolytes

investigation_details.expected_result: hyponatraemia and hyperkalaemia from burn-related fluid shifts and cellular injury
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Electrolyte derangement from fluid shift is missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fluid shifts and tissue injury alter electrolytes; a hospital investigation, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Facial burn + singed nasal hair, hoarse voice/throat tightness, carbonaceous sputum and wheeze together strongly predict inhalation injury and mandate early airway protection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Continuous airway and breathing monitoring pending ambulance
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The airway is the immediate threat; watch for stridor, increasing hoarseness or reduced responsiveness while awaiting paramedics.
Detects carbon-monoxide toxicity that a normal SpO2 would mask — hospital investigation on arrival.
Baseline for fluid resuscitation and to detect burn-related derangements — hospital investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home skin swab or self-directed wound photography for triage
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do not delay 000 escalation to obtain images or samples; this is a time-critical airway emergency, not a wound-care review.
